category_title: Children who exhibit aggressive interpersonal behaviors
minimal_context: The student is asked to tidy up their toys during the transition from free play in the kindergarten classroom.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Free play is over. It is time to tidy up the toys.

STUDENT: No! I am not done playing.

TUTOR: You need to put the toys in the box now.

STUDENT: (Gets angry) I said no! (Snatches a toy from the child sitting next to him)

TUTOR: Please give the toy back and start cleaning up.

STUDENT: (Throws a tantrum) No! (Kicks the toy box over and pushes the other child)

options.A: Use follow-in talk, which refers to talk that is directly related to the student's ongoing actions and attention during the transition.
options.B: Develop joint assessment models, in which the teacher collaborates with psychologists or trained observers to evaluate the student's behavior.
options.C: Adopt a 'leaky' and 'viscous' orientation to control, accepting that the escalating unruliness will begin again, rather than instituting an inflexible regime of disciplinary control.
options.D: Remain available without forcing interaction, first simply sitting near the student and then speaking quietly and gently touching them on the shoulder.
